questions.optionB: Health for All through Primary Health Care — defined health as complete physical, mental, and social well-being, not merely absence of disease
questions.explanation: Core concept: Alma-Ata (1978) = 'Health for All by 2000' through primary healthcare. Why correct: WHO/UNICEF conference at Alma-Ata (Kazakhstan). Key principles: health = fundamental human right; social justice; community participation; intersectoral coordination; appropriate technology; equitable distribution. PHC as the key strategy. Declaration of Astana (2018): renewed PHC commitment. Why A wrong: Alma-Ata emphasized PRIMARY, not tertiary care. Why C wrong: Generalist/comprehensive care, not specialization. Why D wrong: Emphasized government responsibility, not privatization. Clinical relevance: Elements of PHC: education, nutrition, safe water/sanitation, MCH/FP, immunization, local disease control, essential drugs, treatment of common diseases, mental health. 'Health for All' = every person with minimum standard of health. Exam trap: WHO definition of health: 'not merely the absence of disease or infirmity' — social, mental, physical well-being. Memory aid: 'Alma-Ata = All About Primary care.' Reference: Park – Healthcare Delivery.
questions.conceptTag: Alma-Ata PHC Health for All WHO

questions.questionText: Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY provides health insurance cover of:
questions.optionB: ₹5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization — world's largest government-funded health insurance scheme
questions.explanation: Core concept: PM-JAY = ₹5 lakh/family/year health insurance for BPL families. Why correct: Pradhan Mantri-Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY, 2018): ₹5 lakh health insurance per family per year. Covers ~50 crore beneficiaries (10.74 crore poor families). Cashless at empaneled public + private hospitals. Secondary and tertiary hospitalization. No cap on family size, age, or gender. Why A wrong: ₹1 lakh was under RSBY (predecessor scheme). Why C wrong: ₹10 lakh — not the current amount. Why D wrong: Per family, not per person. Clinical relevance: Two components of Ayushman Bharat: (1) HWCs (Health and Wellness Centres — upgraded sub-centres/PHCs for comprehensive PHC) and (2) PM-JAY (insurance). Exam trap: PM-JAY covers HOSPITALIZATION (not OPD visits). HWCs provide comprehensive primary care including CPHC services. Memory aid: 'PM-JAY = 5 lakh JAY (victory over medical costs).' Reference: Park – Health Insurance.
